title: Find the stray process in a setup_all-based suite
prompt: Write `docs/stray_process_hunt.md` describing the hunt for a stray process in this scenario: module `MyApp.BigFeatureTest` has 10 tests. 7 pass reliably. 3 fail only under `mix test --trace`. Trace shows: a `setup_all` block starts a background worker via `GenServer.start_link/3` (unsupervised). Every individual test passes its sandbox checkout cleanly, but mid-test the worker GenServer attempts a Repo.insert and dies with OwnershipError. Must explain: (1) `setup_all` runs in a SEPARATE process from each test process, (2) the GenServer started in setup_all is linked to the setup_all process, (3) that process is alive for the entire module run, (4) its Repo access has NO sandbox allowance from any individual test, (5) fix: move the GenServer start to `setup` (per-test) via `start_supervised!/1` — then each test owns it.
tier_rationale: Three layers deep: setup_all semantics + GenServer lifetime + sandbox per-process ownership.
research_citation: "For us they were caused almost exclusively by async code spawned by liveviews...the test process finished and exited before their async tasks could finish running" — Elixir Forum, egeersoz
